raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/year> : My credit card was charged for three unauthorized transactions of {$7000.00} each, totaling {$21000.00}. The charges were from a company named " XXXX XXXX XXXX  '' in XXXX, XXXX. Note : I was not in XXXX on XX/XX/year>. 

XX/XX/year> : I initiated a dispute/fraud claim with Barclays for these charges. The charges were temporarily removed from my account. 

XX/XX/year> ( Early ) : The charges reappeared on my account. When I contacted Barclays, they informed me that the dispute had been closed, and they had allegedly notified me by phone on XX/XX/year>. I received no such notification via phone, mail, or online portal. 

XX/XX/year> : I sent a formal written dispute letter to Barclays via fax providing substantial evidence that I did not authorize these transactions. That fax is included in this letter. 

XX/XX/year> : Barclays sent a letter, received on XX/XX/XXXX, stating I was responsible for settling the charges with the business. Note : They have not been able to provide me contact information for the business nor can I find them in searching on the internet. 

XX/XX/year> : A Barclays representative informed me my evidence from XX/XX/XXXX was not received until XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, I also received an in-app letter stating the claim would be investigated further. This contradictory timeline raises serious concerns about Barclays ' communication and review process. 

XX/XX/year> : I submitted a copy of my passport with travel stamps, definitively proving I was not in XXXX on the date of the fraudulent transactions. Despite this compelling evidence, Barclays has again denied my claim in writing. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I received a denial in my client portal stating I am responsible for the claims. It listed XXXX  as an agency to consult, so I called on XX/XX/XXXX. 

XXXX XXXX Received a statement from XXXX  stating Barclays denied their request.
